Title: BnaC04.bZIP16 can be phosphorylated and inhibited by BnaA06.SnRK2 and negatively regulates the accumulation of fatty acids in Brassica napus

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BnaC04.bZIP16 is a negative regulator of seed oil accumulation; knockout/editing of BnaC04.bZIP16 and close homologs is a promising strategy to increase seed fatty acid content without obvious penalties in seed size, pollen viability, or yield in Westar. It may be stacked with positive oil genes such as OLEOSIN1, PDH-E1 BETA, WRI1 or with BnaA06.SnRK2 overexpression. Caution: homolog redundancy exists among bZIP16 copies, and effects should be validated in elite high-oil cultivars such as Zhongshuang11.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates seed fatty acid accumulation; overexpression lowers total seed fatty acids and major fatty acid species, whereas CRISPR knockout increases total fatty acids and oleic acid.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; stable overexpression and CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in Brassica napus; seed fatty acid profiling by GC-MS; qRT-PCR; CUT&Tag; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase; Y1H; protein interaction and phosphorylation assays.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds/siliques, especially 15 days after flowering; standard greenhouse/field growth conditions; no special nutrient treatment reported.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing siliques and seeds, especially before/around 15 DAF; among the six homologs, BnaC04.bZIP16 shows the strongest expression.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species because the gene is native to Brassica napus; high in developing siliques and seeds.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BnaA06.SnRK2; BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 promoter; BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA promoter
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: CUT&Tag; LC-MS/MS for phosphosite and interactome identification; no public accession reported.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A G-subfamily bZIP transcription factor. It directly binds G-box motifs in target promoters. Ser151 is a major phosphorylation site. Phosphorylation weakens DNA binding, reduces protein stability, and alters localization from nucleus-only to nucleus plus cytoplasm in the phosphomimic form. The paper notes possible dual regulatory roles on some promoters but the strongest reproducible effect is repression of fatty-acid-related targets.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious adverse agronomic traits reported in transgenic lines; soluble sugars and total proteins in KO seeds were slightly reduced or unchanged; possible off-target effects on carbohydrate metabolism and seed development genes based on CUT&Tag enrichment remain to be explored.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor, G subfamily; basic region plus leucine zipper motif
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; phosphomimic S151D localizes to nucleus and cytoplasm
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC04.bZIP16 is a seed/silique-expressed G-subfamily bZIP TF that negatively regulates seed fatty acid accumulation. 2. It directly binds G-box-containing promoters of BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 and BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA and represses their expression. 3. Overexpression decreases, whereas CRISPR knockout increases, total seed fatty acids and especially oleic acid. 4. BnaA06.SnRK2 phosphorylates BnaC04.bZIP16 at Ser151, reducing its DNA-binding activity and protein stability, thereby relieving repression of fatty acid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage lipids for seed development and major edible oil components for human nutrition and industry.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Seed fatty acid biosynthesis and storage deposition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression of BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 is a practical strategy to raise seed oil/fatty acid content, especially oleic acid, likely by improving oil body formation/storage. It may be stacked with precursor-supply genes such as PDH-E1 BETA or with BnaC04.bZIP16 knockout / BnaA06.SnRK2 overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Does not catalyze a metabolic reaction; structural oil-body protein that coats/stabilizes lipid droplets for triacylglycerol storage.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed fatty acid accumulation; overexpression increases total seed fatty acids and major fatty acid species, especially oleic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CUT&Tag target nomination; dual-luciferase repression assay by BnaC04.bZIP16; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA for promoter binding; stable overexpression in Brassica napus with GC-MS fatty acid profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds at 15 DAF; standard growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in OE-BnaC04.bZIP16 developing seeds; upregulated in OE-BnaA06.SnRK2 seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species because native Brassica napus gene; function examined in developing seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CUT&Tag candidate target; no accession reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not an enzyme, the paper treats it as a direct fatty-acid-related downstream effector because of its established role in oil body formation. Promoter contains G-box motif bound by BnaC04.bZIP16.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oil body formation and neutral lipid storage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear penalty reported for overexpression in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Stabilized oil bodies / lipid droplet storage capacity
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Triacylglycerol-containing oil bodies (structural association rather than catalytic substrate)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; important storage determinant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 is a direct transcriptional target repressed by BnaC04.bZIP16 through a promoter G-box. 2. BnaC04.bZIP16 binding was supported by ChIP-qPCR, Y1H, EMSA, and dual-LUC assays. 3. Stable overexpression of BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 increased total seed fatty acids by about 13%–23% and elevated major fatty acid components, especially oleic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipids for seed energy reserve and edible oil quality.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid biosynthesis precursor supply via pyruvate oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression of BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA can increase seed oil/fatty acid content by strengthening acetyl-CoA generation for plastid fatty acid synthesis. It is a strong candidate for gene stacking with storage genes and relief of repression via BnaC04.bZIP16 editing.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Pyruvate dehydrogenase E1 beta subunit participates in oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate to acetyl-CoA within the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed fatty acid accumulation; overexpression increases total seed fatty acids and major fatty acid species, especially oleic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CUT&Tag target nomination; dual-luciferase repression assay by BnaC04.bZIP16; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA for promoter binding; stable overexpression in Brassica napus with GC-MS fatty acid profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds at 15 DAF; standard growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase E1 component subunit beta
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in OE-BnaC04.bZIP16 developing seeds; upregulated in OE-BnaA06.SnRK2 seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species because native Brassica napus gene; function examined in developing seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CUT&Tag candidate target; no accession reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected for functional validation because of its established role in acetyl-CoA generation and strong consistent regulation. Promoter contains G-box motif bound by BnaC04.bZIP16.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central carbon metabolism to plastid fatty acid synthesis precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear penalty reported for overexpression in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pyruvate dehydrogenase complex E1 beta subunit
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important control point for precursor supply; rate-limiting status not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely plastid/plastidial PDH complex; not directly tested in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA is a direct target repressed by BnaC04.bZIP16 through G-box promoter binding. 2. Direct regulation was validated by ChIP-qPCR, Y1H, EMSA, and dual-LUC assays. 3. Overexpression of BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA raised seed total fatty acids by about 13%–20% and increased major fatty acid components, especially oleic acid, consistent with enhanced acetyl-CoA supply.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipids and major edible oil constituents.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Because BnaA06.SnRK2 overexpression increases seed fatty acid content by antagonizing the repressor BnaC04.bZIP16, it is a promising engineering target for oil improvement. It may be combined with BnaC04.bZIP16 editing or downstream pathway/storage genes. Caution: as SnRK2 kinases often participate in stress/ABA signaling, broader pleiotropic effects should be monitored in breeding backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed fatty acid accumulation; overexpression increases total fatty acid content and oleic acid, apparently by phosphorylating and inhibiting BnaC04.bZIP16.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LC-MS/MS candidate identification; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; yeast two-hybrid; in vitro kinase assay; phosphosite mutagenesis; cell-free degradation assay; RT-qPCR of downstream genes; overexpression in Brassica napus with seed GC-MS profiling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-setting upstream kinase that relieves repression of fatty acid biosynthesis/storage genes by phosphorylating the core repressor BnaC04.bZIP16; a positive controller of seed fatty acid accumulation in this module.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds before 45 DAF, especially 15 DAF; standard growth conditions; no external ABA/stress treatment tested, though discussion links SnRK2 to stress signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression pattern highly coincides with BnaC04.bZIP16 before 45 DAF during seed development.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species because native Brassica napus gene; active in developing seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedback loop suggested because BnaA06.SnRK2 transcript was upregulated in OE-BnaC04.bZIP16 lines; whether direct or indirect remains unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of fatty acid biosynthesis and storage via post-translational inhibition of a repressor
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS interactome/phosphosite analysis; no accession reported.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A serine/threonine kinase with high identity to Arabidopsis SnRK2.5. It phosphorylates BnaC04.bZIP16 mainly at Ser151, decreasing DNA binding to target promoters, reducing protein stability through the ubiquitin/26S proteasome pathway, and altering subcellular localization of phosphomimic BnaC04.bZIP16.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No agronomic penalty reported here, but potential pleiotropy with abiotic stress and ABA signaling is plausible and should be evaluated.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BnaC04.bZIP16 (direct substrate); indirectly derepresses BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 and BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in kinase-substrate interaction with BnaC04.bZIP16; no stable larger complex established.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation-mediated inhibition and destabilization of a transcriptional repressor
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly activates BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 and BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA by phosphorylating BnaC04.bZIP16 and weakening its repression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA06.SnRK2 physically interacts with BnaC04.bZIP16 in nucleus-associated assays and phosphorylates it in vitro and in vivo. 2. Ser151 is a key phosphorylation site; phosphorylation reduces BnaC04.bZIP16 DNA-binding activity, stability, and strict nuclear localization. 3. Overexpression of BnaA06.SnRK2 increases expression of BnaA08.OLEOSIN1 and BnaC03.PDH-E1 BETA without changing BnaC04.bZIP16 transcript level, and increases seed fatty acid content by at least ~20%.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipids important for seed energy reserve and edible oil value.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Not directly tested; as a SnRK2, likely linked to ABA and abiotic stress signaling by family context.
